class.name: FairlyDocumented
class.deprecated: we will use normal integration tests with editor in the future.
class.experimental: both experimental and deprecated tags are [b]experimental[/b].
class.brief_description:
[i]documented[/i] ~ [b]documented[/b] ~ [AABB] < [url=https://github.com/godot-rust/gdext/pull/748]pr[/url] This is a paragraph. It has some text in it. It's a paragraph. It's quite long, and wraps multiple lines. It is describing the struct [code]Player[/code]. Or maybe perhaps it's describing the module. It's hard to say, really. It even has some code in it: [code]let x = 5;[/code]. And some more code: [code]let y = 6;[/code]. And a bunch of [b]bold[/b] and [i]italic[/i] text with [i]different[/i] ways to do it. Don't forget about [url=https://example.com]links[/url]. a few tests: headings: # Some heading lists: - lists - like this - with sublists that are multiline - and subsublists - and list items * maybe with [code]*[/code] as well [reference-style link][somelink] links with back-references: Blah blah[^foo] Also same reference[^foo] [^foo]: [url=https://example.org]https://example.org[/url] footnotes: We cannot florbinate the glorb[^florb] [^florb]: because the glorb doesn't flibble. Third note in order of use[^1] and fourth [^bignote] [^1]: This is the third footnote in order of definition. [^bignote]: Fourth footnote in order of definition. [^biggernote]: This is the fifth footnote in order of definition. Fifth note in order of use. [^someothernote] [^someothernote]: sixth footnote in order of definition. Sixth footnote in order of use. [^biggernote] task lists: We must ensure that we've completed - [ ] task 1 - [x] task 2 tables: | Header1 | Header2 | |---------|---------| | abc | def | images: [url=https://godotengine.org/assets/press/logo_small_color_light.png]https://godotengine.org/assets/press/logo_small_color_light.png[/url] ![Image][image] blockquotes: > Some cool thing ordered list: 1. thing one 2. thing two 1. thing two point one 2. thing two point two 3. thing two point three Something here < this is technically header syntax --- And here smart punctuation codeblocks: [code]rust #![no_main] #[link_section=".text"] #[no_mangle] static main: u64 = 0x31c0678b10; [/code] [code]gdscript extends Node func _ready(): print("Hello, world!") [/code] [code]csharp using Godot; public class Player : Node2D { [Export] public float Speed = 400.0f; } [/code] Some HTML to make sure it's properly escaped:
<- this is inline HTML
<- not considered HTML (manually escaped) [code]inline
code[/code] [code]html
code block
[/code] [url=https://www.google.com/search?q=2+%2B+2+<+5]Google: 2 + 2 < 5[/url] connect these [somelink]: [url=https://example.com]https://example.com[/url] [image]: [url=https://godotengine.org/assets/press/logo_small_color_dark.png]https://godotengine.org/assets/press/logo_small_color_dark.png[/url]

method.name: process_node
method.description: Function with lots of special characters ([code]Gd<Node>[/code])

method.name: _virtual_documented
method.description: some virtual function that should be overridden by a user some multiline doc The [code]Gd<Node>[/code] param should be properly escaped

method.name: experimental_method
method.description: This is a method. Who knows

method.name: deprecated_method
method.deprecated: EXPLODES ON USE DO NOT USE

constant.name: A
constant.deprecated: Did you know that constants can be deprecated?

constant.name: B
constant.experimental: Did you know that constants can be experimental?

constants.constant: this docstring has < a special character

signal.name: documented_signal
signal.description: some user signal some multiline doc The [code]Gd<Node>[/code] param should be properly escaped

signal.name: other_signal
signal.experimental: this is new signal use it at your own risk

members.member: not to be confused with B!
this docstring has < a special character
